question: Design a base class `Publication` with private attributes `title` (string) and `price` (double). Implement methods to set and display these. From `Publication`, derive `Book` (adds private attribute `author` - string) and `Magazine` (adds private attribute `issueNumber` - int). Demonstrate creating objects of `Book` and `Magazine` and displaying their complete details.

test_cases.input:
The C++ Programming Language
69.99
Bjarne Stroustrup
C++ Magazine
12.50
25
test_cases.expected:
--- Enter Book Details ---
Enter book title: Enter book price: Enter book author: 
--- Enter Magazine Details ---
Enter magazine title: Enter magazine price: Enter magazine issue number: 
--- Displaying Details ---

Book Details:
Title: The C++ Programming Language
Price: $69.99
Author: Bjarne Stroustrup

Magazine Details:
Title: C++ Magazine
Price: $12.5
Issue Number: 25

test_cases.input:
Invalid Book
abc
Author Name
Invalid Magazine
def
xyz
test_cases.expected:
--- Enter Book Details ---
Enter book title: Enter book price: Enter book author: 
--- Enter Magazine Details ---
Enter magazine title: Enter magazine price: Enter magazine issue number: 
--- Displaying Details ---

Book Details:
Title: Invalid Book
Price: $0
Author: Author Name

Magazine Details:
Title: Invalid Magazine
Price: $0
Issue Number: 0

errors: Lack of input validation for numeric fields (price, issue number). If non-numeric input is provided, `std::cin` enters a fail state, leading to default-initialized values (0) and potential issues with subsequent input operations.
The program does not explicitly check `std::cin.fail()` after numeric input, which means it proceeds with potentially invalid data without informing the user or attempting recovery.
